news.summary: NASA has unveiled its plans to send humans to the Moon, which include establishing a permanent presence on Earth's nearest satellite by the 2030s. It comes as China moves ahead with its own lunar ambitions, launching three astronauts into outer space. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks to Michelle L.

news.headline: NCCIA Punjab arrests 11 for 'anti-state propaganda' and 'inciting unrest' online
news.summary:
<p>LAHORE: The National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) has arrested 11 ‘social media activists’ in different cities of Punjab for allegedly spreading anti-state propaganda and inciting unrest among the public.</p>

news.headline: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
news.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

news.headline: Pressure Grows on Spain’s Leader After Police Search His Party Office
news.summary: After a judge accused his party of corruption, Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez of Spain has faced mounting calls to resign.

news.headline: Crackdown on culture: Israel censoring Palestinian voices
news.summary: A United Nations commission, Amnesty International and Human Rights Watch have all separately concluded that Israel has committed genocide in Gaza, where tens of thousands of Palestinians have been killed since the start of the war in October 2023. news.headline: Israel carries out strike on Beirut suburbs, first near capital in weeks
news.summary: An Israeli strike hit a building in the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital on Thursday, the first strike to hit near Beirut in weeks amid a ceasefire that has failed to halt fighting between Israeli troops and Hezbollah in south ​Lebanon. news.headline: Brazil's Lula backs new oil, gas drilling in western Amazon
news.summary: Brazilian oil giant Petrobras announced Wednesday the drilling of new wells at an oil and gas field in the Amazon rainforest for the first time in a decade, at an event attended by President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva. news.headline: Gaza children with Down syndrome are still reckoning with the devastation of the war
news.summary: The war in Gaza has especially affected the most vulnerable groups in the enclave; among them are children with Down syndrome. news.headline: Reconnected? Iranians say only 'drip-feed' internet has returned after three-month shutdown
news.summary: After almost three months of being cut off from the world, internet access was partially restored in Iran starting on Tuesday.
